metadata.comparison: Current PLC AAS (NodeSet/reports) vs Target AAS models

datatype_mismatches.recommendation: Target prefers Int for buffer count; PLC uses String - keep String for S7-1200 or add Int variable

TARGET_CELL_AAS.access_level_mismatches: All PLC variables are ReadWrite; target Cell Identification/Status/Process are Read. Optional: restrict static config to Read if desired.
TARGET_CELL_AAS.synchronization_gaps: None for Cell-only; Cell data is PLC-owned. Queue methods (FreeFromPosition, FreeFromQueue, GetStatus, GetSupported, ReserveAction) exist and align with target methods.

missing_elements.description: Dedicated NFC UID string (PLC has only CurrentId/ID)

extra_elements_in_plc.note: Dual use (Cell ID vs Product ID); target separates Cell.ID and Product.ProductID

datatype_mismatches.note: Reader must write TagUID/ProductID as string

TARGET_PRODUCT_AAS.access_level_mismatches: Target marks ProductID, TagUID, State, Timestamp, ErrorCode, CurrentId, CurrentPosition as writable by reader; PLC already ReadWrite - OK.
TARGET_PRODUCT_AAS.synchronization_gaps: PLC has variables but reader currently does NOT write CurrentId, CurrentPosition, ActionStatus (per existing reports). Implementing reader writes to these nodes would close the gap.

extra_elements_in_plc.note: Single String for status; target splits into Ready/Busy/Done/Fault/ErrorCode
Method exists; target Transport optional has no explicit method, ReserveAction is Cell-level

rename_candidates.suggested_rename: Keep for backward compatibility or add Transport/Ready, Busy, Done, Fault, ErrorCode and deprecate ActionStatus string

datatype_mismatches.recommendation: Target uses separate Bool/Int nodes; PLC uses one String. Either extend PLC with new nodes or encode in ActionStatus string.

TARGET_TRANSPORT_AAS.access_level_mismatches: Target Transport CmdStart/CmdReset/TaskID are ReadWrite (PLC); Ready/Busy/Done/Fault/ErrorCode are Read - PLC can expose all as ReadWrite for simplicity.
TARGET_TRANSPORT_AAS.synchronization_gaps: Transport AAS is PLC-only; no reader sync required. If robot is controlled by same PLC, adding Transport.* nodes (or clarifying ActionStatus semantics) aligns with target.

summary.cell: PLC has most Cell inputs (ID, Name, InputPosition, OutputPosition, Queue*, methods). Missing: Status_System (Ready, Fault, ErrorCode), SupportedProcessTypes. Some types String vs Int.
summary.product: PLC has ID, CurrentId, CurrentPosition, Name, Submodel. Missing: TagUID, RecipeID_or_VariantID, State, Timestamp, ErrorCode, SchemaVersion. Reader must write ProductID/CurrentPosition to close sync gap.
summary.transport: PLC has ActionStatus (String) and ReserveAction. Target expects separate Ready/Busy/Done/Fault/ErrorCode (Bool/Int). Missing dedicated Transport command/status nodes unless encoded in ActionStatus.
